title: practical english usage - Part 35
content:
-  Negative questions (►   218): do I not work? or don’ t I work? etc -  Contracted negatives (►  337): I don’ t work, he doesn’t work, etc they work             do they work?              they do not work we  work                do we  work?                we  do not work he/she/it works    does he/she/it work?    he/she/it does not work you work              do you work?                you do not work I work                   do I work?                     I do not work Affirmative  Question                 Negative 1  forms 30 simple     present:     forms grammar •  31   simple present: use that refer to the future. (For details, ►    231).

And the simple present is often used instead of will... in subordinate clauses His train arrives at 11.46.  I start my new job tomorrow.

However, the simple present is used for 'timetabled’  future events (►  37.1).

'There's the doorbell.’ 'I'll get it.’ (     I get it.) (►  79.1) not We're going to the theatre this evening, (     We go to the theatre-this evening.) not I promise I won't smoke any more, (     I promise I don't smoke  ...) (►  79.3) We   do not normally use the simple present to talk about the future.

4 talking     about     the   future not                                                                           not I like this wine, (     I'm liking...) I believe you. (     I'm-believing yen.) verbs that do not usually have progressive forms (►  4).

However, the simple present is used for this 'around the present’  meaning with 3  non-progressive           verbs not 'Where's Bernard?1 'He's playing tennis.' (     .. .-He ploys tennis.) - I play tennis every Wednesday.

Look - it's snowing! (     Leek— it snews!) - It usually snows in January.

The kettle's boiling - shall I make tea? (     The kettle beds...) - Water boils at 100°  Celsius.

actions that are only going on around the present. Compare: We   do not usually use the simple present to talk about temporary situations or 2 not   used    for   things     happening        just   around      the   present ►    PAST                                       NOW                       FUTURE I play tennis every Wednesday.

simple     present:     things     that    happen      repeatedly simple     present:     permanent        situations It always rains here in November. I play tennis every Wednesday.

Alice works for an insurance company.

grammar •  32   present progressive on   now    or  around      now present      progressive:       temporary        actions     and   situations       going He's working in Saudi Arabia at the moment.

‘ What are you doing? 'I'm writing emails, (     .../ write emails.)’ not Hurry up! We're all waiting for you! (     We all wait...) on now or 'around now': before, during and after the moment of speaking.

We   use the present progressive to talk about temporary situations that are going 2  use:  'around     now' For spelling of -ing forms. ►    346-347.    For passive forms (e.g. The work is being done), ►    57.

1 am waiting. Are you listening? She isn't working today.

lights, then you turn left,../ 'How do I get to the station? ' You go straight on until you come to the traffic Lydiard passes to Taylor, Taylor shoots - and it's a goal!

First I take a bowl and break two eggs into it. (     First I am  taking...) instructions and present-tense stories (►  33   for more details).

simple present. This happens, for example, in demonstrations, commentaries, When  we  talk about a series of completed actions and events, we  often use the stories 5  series     of  events:    demonstrations,           commentaries,          instructions, Why don't you take a day off tomorrow?
